title: arg_session: Ensure created void* types have arch.
body:
This PR fixes the following exception:

```python
File F:\angr\angr\angr\calling_conventions.py:878, in SimCC.arg_locs(self, prototype)
    876 if prototype._arch is None:
    877     prototype = prototype.with_arch(self.arch)
--> 878 session = self.arg_session(prototype.returnty)
    879 return [self.next_arg(session, arg_ty) for arg_ty in prototype.args]

File F:\angr\angr\angr\calling_conventions.py:728, in SimCC.arg_session(self, ret_ty)
    726 session = self.ArgSession(self)
    727 if self.return_in_implicit_outparam(ret_ty):
--> 728     self.next_arg(session, SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()))
    729 return session

File F:\angr\angr\angr\calling_conventions.py:1614, in SimCCSystemVAMD64.next_arg(self, session, arg_type)
   1612     arg_type = SimTypePointer(arg_type.elem_type).with_arch(self.arch)
   1613 state = session.getstate()
-> 1614 classification = self._classify(arg_type)
   1615 try:
   1616     mapped_classes = []

File F:\angr\angr\angr\calling_conventions.py:1678, in SimCCSystemVAMD64._classify(self, ty, chunksize)
   1676     chunksize = self.arch.bytes
   1677 # treat BOT as INTEGER
-> 1678 nchunks = 1 if isinstance(ty, SimTypeBottom) else (ty.size // self.arch.byte_width + chunksize - 1) // chunksize
   1679 if isinstance(ty, (SimTypeFloat,)):
   1680     return ["SSE"] + ["SSEUP"] * (nchunks - 1)

File F:\angr\angr\angr\sim_type.py:778, in SimTypePointer.size(self)
    775 @property
    776 def size(self):
    777     if self._arch is None:
--> 778         raise ValueError("Can't tell my size without an arch!")
    779     return self._arch.bits

ValueError: Can't tell my size without an arch!
```

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fix a crash in calling convention argument layout by ensuring internally synthesized implicit-outparam pointer types (void*/BOT*) are created with the current architecture before any size/classification logic runs, especially for SysV AMD64.

steps.operation: Attach the calling convention arch to the synthesized implicit sret/outparam pointer type before passing it to next_arg()
steps.reason: SimCC.arg_session() currently constructs SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) without an arch, but downstream CC implementations (e.g., SysV AMD64) may query ty.size during classification/layout, which raises when _arch is None. Creating the temporary void*/BOT* with .with_arch(self.arch) ensures .size is always available and preserves ABI behavior.
steps.side_effects: May slightly change internal type objects (they become arch-bound) but should not change computed arg locations/ABI decisions; prevents ValueError in arg_locs() for implicit-outparam returns. Any tests that assert exact repr/identity of the synthesized type may need updates.

steps.operation: Audit and fix any similar implicit-outparam construction in return value handling by ensuring synthesized pointer types are arch-bound
steps.reason: SimCC.return_val() has an implicit-outparam-related path that can also synthesize a SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) (or similar placeholder) and may trigger the same missing-arch size query in some return-path layouts. Normalizing the internally created type with self.arch prevents latent crashes beyond the arg_session() traceback.
steps.side_effects: Could affect return-value layout code paths that previously relied on arch-less placeholder types; should be behavior-preserving for ABI, but may surface other callers that were unintentionally passing arch-less types around.

steps.operation: Optionally add a defensive normalization in SysV AMD64 next_arg() for any arch-less arg types before classification
steps.reason: Even with the primary fix, other call sites may still pass arch-less SimTypes into next_arg(). Adding a narrow guard (e.g., if arg_type._arch is None: arg_type = arg_type.with_arch(self.arch)) makes SysV AMD64 robust and prevents future regressions where _classify() triggers ty.size on arch-less types.
steps.side_effects: Slightly broadens behavior by auto-attaching arch in this CC’s argument mapping; should be safe, but could hide bugs where callers expected an exception for missing arch. May require adjusting or adding tests for normalization behavior.

steps.operation: Ensure classification never reads .size from an arch-less type (validate/normalize at entry, or provide clearer error)
steps.reason: _classify() directly computes nchunks using ty.size and will raise if ty lacks arch. Adding a small entry normalization/validation (preferably normalization to self.arch when possible) makes the failure mode impossible (or at least clearer) and complements the upstream fix to keep the classifier resilient.
steps.side_effects: May change exception behavior (fewer ValueErrors; possibly different error messages). If normalization is added here, it could overlap with next_arg()’s guard; ensure no double-wrapping or unintended arch overriding occurs.
